rootCause.summary: The client-side application sent an empty email address to the /api/validate endpoint, which resulted in a 400 Bad Request due to server-side validation failure.
triggerChain: User likely interacted with a form or input field intended for email submission/validation.
The client-side code constructed a request body with an empty string for the 'email' field.
A POST request was sent to /api/validate with the malformed request body.
The server responded with a 400 status code and a validation error message indicating that a valid email address is required.
evidence: Response body snippet: {"error":"VALIDATION_ERROR","path":"/api/validate","message":"A valid email address is required."}
rootCause.fix: Implement client-side validation to prevent submitting an empty email address to the `/api/validate` endpoint. Ensure the email input field is properly populated or validated before the API call is made.

bugReport.title: API validation fails for empty email submission
bugReport.description: When an empty email address is submitted to the `/api/validate` endpoint, the server responds with a 400 Bad Request. The response body indicates a `VALIDATION_ERROR` with the message 'A valid email address is required.', confirming that the server rejected the request due to an invalid (empty) email value. This suggests a lack of client-side validation or an issue with how the email input's value is being captured before the API call.
timeline: 1776259370371: POST request to /api/validate initiated with an empty email field.
1776259370371: Server responded with 400 Bad Request to /api/validate.
bugReport.networkActivity: One failed network call: POST /api/validate returned 400 Bad Request. Request body: {"email":""}. Response body: {"error":"VALIDATION_ERROR","path":"/api/validate","message":"A valid email address is required."}
bugReport.consoleLogs: No console errors or warnings were recorded.
bugReport.environment: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/147.0.0.0 Safari/537.36, Page URL: http://localhost:8090/, Screen Size: Not available in metadata.

steps: Locate the email input field (or the UI component that triggers email validation).
Attempt to submit the form or trigger the validation without entering any value into the email field (or by entering an empty string).
reproductionSteps.expectedBehavior: The client-side application should prevent the submission of an empty email address, either by displaying a client-side validation error or by disabling the submit button until a valid email is entered. The API should not be called with an empty email.
reproductionSteps.actualBehavior: The client-side application sends a POST request to /api/validate with an empty email address, and the server responds with a 400 Bad Request due to validation failure.

serverLogSummary.summary: Server logs were not available for analysis as the configured server log file was not found.
